interpretation: Under +50% mass at seed 2026: single-source-wide R0_pd_s5 (K=10 PD-Gaussian σ=5) achieves 32/40 = 80.0%, slightly higher than the two-source R1_s5 at 29/40 = 72.5%. McNemar R0-vs-R1 is statistically inconclusive (see tests). The result reframes the methodological story: the dominant lever is the wide noise scale σ=5 in either configuration, not the two-source partition per se.
